creator: Ram Iyer
title: (No AI) Applied Computing wants to give oil and gas operators an AI model for the entire plant

content: Applied Computing has raised a $20M Series A to build a foundation AI model for the oil, gas and petrochemical industry.

creator: Lucas Ropek
title: (No AI) Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic

title: (No AI) xAI sues a man for using Grok to generate CSAM ‘deepfakes’
author: Emma Roth
content:
![Vector illustration of the xAI logo.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/25362103/STK262_GROK_XAI__A.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

The Elon Musk-owned xAI is suing a South Carolina man who allegedly used the company's Grok AI chatbot to generate child sexual abuse material (CSAM). In [a lawsuit](https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/28483528-spacexai-v-harwood/) reported [earlier by _Reuters_](https://www.reuters.com/legal/litigation/musks-xai-sues-grok-user-over-sexualized-deepfakes-2026-07-15/), xAI claims Terry Wayne Harwood "knowingly and intentionally used Grok to circumvent safeguards, alter nonconsensual images, and generate and distribute CSAM," breaching the company's policies.

Harwood [was arrested](https://www.scag.gov/about-the-office/news/four-upstate-men-arrested-on-child-sexual-abuse-material-charges/) in February for allegedly possessing and distributing CSAM and is facing eight felony charges. The lawsuit claims "at least some" of the images related to Harwood's criminal charges "were generated or altered" with Grok, and that Harwood byp …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/966293/xai-grok-user-lawsuit-csam)
contentSnippet:
The Elon Musk-owned xAI is suing a South Carolina man who allegedly used the company's Grok AI chatbot to generate child sexual abuse material (CSAM). In a lawsuit reported earlier by Reuters, xAI claims Terry Wayne Harwood "knowingly and intentionally used Grok to circumvent safeguards, alter nonconsensual images, and generate and distribute CSAM," breaching the company's policies.
Harwood was arrested in February for allegedly possessing and distributing CSAM and is facing eight felony charges. The lawsuit claims "at least some" of the images related to Harwood's criminal charges "were generated or altered" with Grok, and that Harwood byp …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: The Elon Musk-owned xAI is suing a South Carolina man who allegedly used the company's Grok AI chatbot to generate child sexual abuse material (CSAM). In a lawsuit reported earlier by Reuters, xAI claims Terry Wayne Harwood "knowingly and intentionally used Grok to circumvent safeguards, alter nonconsensual images, and generate and distribute CSAM," breaching the […]

title: (No AI) AI slop movies are the new direct-to-video cash grabs
author: Charles Pulliam-Moore
content:
![An AI-generated image of a Trojan soldier.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Screenshot-2026-07-14-at-3.55.09-PM.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

This weekend, cinephiles across the world will march to their local theaters to feast their eyes on Christopher Nolan's new adaptation of [_The Odyssey_](https://www.theverge.com/entertainment/849026/the-trailer-for-christopher-nolans-the-odyssey-dropped-this-morning)_._ It's on track to [rake in anywhere between $80-$100 million](https://deadline.com/2026/06/the-odyssey-box-office-opening-projection-1236968681/) in just a few days. People are clearly excited to see [how Nolan uses cutting-edge filmmaking tech](https://www.theverge.com/news/668287/christopher-nolan-odyssey-imax-film-100-percent) to make the Homeric classic feel fresh. But another director is trying to capitalize on the buzz around Nolan's project to drum up interest in an Odysseus-focused movie of his own.

On Tuesday, [film studio Fountain 0 announced](https://watch.fountain0.com/movies/odysseus-the-fall) that it is working on an AI-generated reimagining of _The Odyssey_ titled _Odysseus: The Fall_, which will be availabl …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/entertainment/965616/ash-koosha-odysseus-the-fall-foundtain-zero-tilly-norwood)

It's on track to rake in anywhere between $80-$100 million in just a few days. People are clearly excited to see how Nolan uses cutting-edge filmmaking tech to make the Homeric classic feel fresh. But another director is trying to capitalize on the buzz around Nolan's project to drum up interest in an Odysseus-focused movie of his own.
On Tuesday, film studio Fountain 0 announced that it is working on an AI-generated reimagining of The Odyssey titled Odysseus: The Fall, which will be availabl …

title: (No AI) The US is advancing AI safety through state and federal action
content: OpenAI outlines a “reverse federalism” approach to AI governance, where state laws help build a national framework for safe, democratic AI.
contentSnippet: OpenAI outlines a “reverse federalism” approach to AI governance, where state laws help build a national framework for safe, democratic AI.

title: (No AI) GPT-Red: Unlocking Self-Improvement for Robustness
content: Explore GPT-Red, OpenAI’s automated red teaming system that uses self-play to improve AI safety, alignment, and prompt injection robustness.

title: (No AI) Empowering India’s next generation of innovators with ATL Saathi

creator: Reece Rogers
title: (No AI) Please Stop Making Me Opt Out of AI
dc:creator: Reece Rogers
content: I’m sick of “opt-out” toggles for automatically enabled generative AI features. It’s past time to make “opt in” the default setting for sensitive features.
contentSnippet: I’m sick of “opt-out” toggles for automatically enabled generative AI features. It’s past time to make “opt in” the default setting for sensitive features.

creator: Will Knight
title: (No AI) AI Isn’t Smarter Than a Baby—Yet
dc:creator: Will Knight
content: Babies are tremendous learning machines, and key advances for AI may soon be found in the architecture of their little brains.
contentSnippet: Babies are tremendous learning machines, and key advances for AI may soon be found in the architecture of their little brains.
